title: hylotelephium maximum subsp. ruprechtii
intro: Hylotelephium maximum subsp. ruprechtii, commonly known as 'Ruprecht's Stonecrop', is a type of succulent plant belonging to the Crassulaceae family. It is known for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The therapeutic actions of Hylotelephium maximum subsp. ruprechtii include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor activities, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. However, I don't have specific information on the bioactive constituents of this plant, but it's known to have various therapeutic properties.

side_effects: Hylotelephium maximum subsp. ruprechtii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems due to its high saponin content.
